message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  Every USA wave, including this latest Spring 2022 Resurgence has been successfully modeled using:
                  <jats:disp-formula id="ueqn1">
                    <jats:alternatives>
                      <jats:graphic xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" xlink:href="22276889v1_ueqn1.gif" position="float" orientation="portrait"/>
                    </jats:alternatives>
                  </jats:disp-formula>
                  with
                  <jats:italic>N</jats:italic>
                  (
                  <jats:italic>t</jats:italic>
                  ) being the total number of new CoVID-19 cases above a prior baseline, and
                  <jats:italic>t</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  setting the pandemic wave doubling time
                  <jats:italic>t</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>dbl</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  =
                  <jats:italic>t</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  (In 2). The parameters {
                  <jats:italic>α</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>S</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  ;
                  <jats:italic>δ</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>o</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  } measure mitigation efforts among the
                  <jats:italic>uninfected population</jats:italic>
                  . Here, {
                  <jats:italic>α</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>S</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  <jats:italic>></jats:italic>
                  0} is associated with
                  <jats:italic>Social Distancing</jats:italic>
                  and
                  <jats:italic>vaccinations</jats:italic>
                  ; while {
                  <jats:italic>δ</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>o</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  <jats:italic>></jats:italic>
                  0} is associated with
                  <jats:italic>mask-wearing</jats:italic>
                  , which results in faster
                  <jats:inline-formula>
                    <jats:alternatives>
                      <jats:inline-graphic xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" xlink:href="22276889v1_inline1.gif"/>
                    </jats:alternatives>
                  </jats:inline-formula>
                  post-peak
                  <jats:italic>drop-offs</jats:italic>
                  . The predicted pandemic wave end is when the calculated
                  <jats:italic>N</jats:italic>
                  (
                  <jats:italic>t</jats:italic>
                  ) stops increasing.
                </jats:p>
                <jats:p>
                  The USA Winter 2021 Resurgence resulted in fewer
                  <jats:italic>Omicron</jats:italic>
                  CoVID-19 cases than calculated in our prior
                  <jats:italic>medrxiv</jats:italic>
                  .
                  <jats:italic>org</jats:italic>
                  preprint
                  <jats:sup>*</jats:sup>
                  , due to an increased
                  <jats:italic>δ</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>o</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  component, which gives ∼3
                  <jats:italic>/</jats:italic>
                  11
                  <jats:italic>/</jats:italic>
                  22 as the predicted wave end. A relatively quiet CoVID-19 period ensued until ∼4
                  <jats:italic>/</jats:italic>
                  16
                  <jats:italic>/</jats:italic>
                  22, when a new
                  <jats:italic>Omicron</jats:italic>
                  variant caused the present Spring 2022 CoVID-19 resurgence.
                </jats:p>
                <jats:p>
                  The recent CoVID-19 waves have decreasing
                  <jats:italic>t</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  values, with the Spring 2022
                  <jats:italic>t</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  <jats:italic>≈</jats:italic>
                  3.55
                  <jats:italic>days</jats:italic>
                  (
                  <jats:italic>t</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>dbl</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  ≈ 2.46
                  <jats:italic>days</jats:italic>
                  ) value being the shortest since the initial 2020 pandemic, indicating increasingly infectious variants. The Winter 2021 and the present Spring 2022 CoVID-19 resurgences have identical
                  <jats:italic>α</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>S</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  ≈ 0.043
                  <jats:italic>/ day</jats:italic>
                  values, but the
                  <jats:italic>δ</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>o</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  <jats:italic>></jats:italic>
                  0
                  <jats:italic>mask-wearing</jats:italic>
                  parameter decreased from
                  <jats:italic>δ</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>o</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  ≈ 3. 14 × 10
                  <jats:sup>−3</jats:sup>
                  <jats:italic>/ day</jats:italic>
                  to
                  <jats:italic>δ</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>o</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  ≈ 1. 145 × 10
                  <jats:sup>−3</jats:sup>
                  <jats:italic>/ day</jats:italic>
                  , giving the Spring 2022 wave a longer tail, and an expected end date of 8/25/22, with these wave totals:
                  <jats:disp-formula id="ueqn2">
                    <jats:alternatives>
                      <jats:graphic xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" xlink:href="22276889v1_ueqn2.gif" position="float" orientation="portrait"/>
                    </jats:alternatives>
                  </jats:disp-formula>
                </jats:p>
                <jats:p>
                  When all the USA CoVID-19 waves are combined, it gives:
                  <jats:disp-formula id="ueqn3">
                    <jats:alternatives>
                      <jats:graphic xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" xlink:href="22276889v1_ueqn3.gif" position="float" orientation="portrait"/>
                    </jats:alternatives>
                  </jats:disp-formula>
                  assuming no future CoVID-19 Resurgences (
                  <jats:italic>with 5 Figures</jats:italic>
                  ).
                </jats:p>
                <jats:p>
                  <jats:italic>*(10</jats:italic>
                  .
                  <jats:italic>1101_2022</jats:italic>
                  .
                  <jats:italic>02</jats:italic>
                  .
                  <jats:italic>04</jats:italic>
                  .
                  <jats:italic>22270491)</jats:italic>
                </jats:p>
title: Modeling the USA Winter 2021 Update and Spring 2022 CoVID-19 Resurgence

reference.unstructured: https://medrxiv.orgcgicontentshort2020.05.04.20091207v1 “Initial Model for the Impact of Social Distancing on CoVID-19 Spread”
https://medrxiv.orgcgicontentshort2020.06.30.20143149v1 “Orthogonal Functions for Evaluating Social Distancing Impact on CoVID-19 Spread”
https://medrxiv.orgcgicontentshort2020.0.07.20169904 “Model to Describe Fast Shutoff of CoVID-19 Pandemic Spread”
https://medrxiv.orgcgicontentshort2021.0.16.21262150 “The IHME vs Me: Modeling USA CoVID-19 Spread, Early Data to the Fifth Wave”
https://medrxiv.orgcgicontentshort2021.10.15.2126507 “Updated Model for the USA Summer 2021 CoVID-19 Resurgence”
